compiled_state.title: Test gate should be merge-aware: skip re-running tests for files from main
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
When merging from main into a worktree branch, the test gate requires re-running tests for ALL files changed by the merge — including files that were already tested on main. This creates unnecessary friction during merge-to-main.sh: the pre-commit hook blocks the merge commit because record-test-status.sh hasn't run for files that came in from main.

The test gate should detect merge commits (MERGE_HEAD exists) and either: (1) skip the test gate entirely for merge commits (similar to the review gate's merge-aware behavior), or (2) only require tests for files that were modified on BOTH sides (actual conflict resolution), not files that came in cleanly from main.

Observed during: epic d775-4a36 session close, merging 16 commits with 92 files from main. Required multiple rounds of record-test-status.sh + re-commit attempts.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC). Root cause area: pre-commit-test-gate.sh lacks MERGE_HEAD detection that pre-commit-review-gate.sh already has.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
